reviews.author: Emil Sjöholm
reviews.text: Love this spot. You're able to see both the major beaches from here. Downside is you have to pay (not much), lots of tourists and it closes at 6pm already. If you like scenic spots and water; do go! Otherwise you might as well skip this one.

reviews.author: Akang
reviews.text: Natural rocks overlooking the sea, a great spot for picture taking, especially for wedding photos. Chill with a cup of coffee at the neaby coffee shop with sea view and breeze, life is good.

reviews.author: Diễm Hằng Phạm
reviews.text: Hòn Chồng Nha Trang (also known as Hòn Chồng – Hòn Vợ or Hòn Chồng Beach) is one of the most unique and romantic natural attractions in Nha Trang. Located in Vĩnh Phước Ward, about 4–5 km north of the city center (along Phạm Văn Đồng Street), it’s a must-visit spot for anyone who loves dramatic rock formations by the sea. Location & Accessibility Easy to reach by motorbike, taxi, or Grab from central Nha Trang (10–15 minutes). The site is right by the sea with parking available. Entrance fee is around 22,000–30,000 VND per person (very affordable). Open daily from 7:00 AM to 7:00 PM. Scenery & Atmosphere The highlight is the bizarre, naturally stacked giant rocks forming unique shapes, including the legendary “Husband” and “Wife” rocks from a touching local folktale. Climb up the rocks for stunning panoramic views of Nha Trang Bay, Hòn Tre Island, and the endless blue sea—especially magical at sunrise or sunset! The breeze is refreshing, waves crash gently, and the vibe is super romantic and relaxing, perfect for couples or friends taking dreamy photos. There are seaside cafés right in the area with reasonable prices (drinks 18,000–70,000 VND). You can sit, sip coffee, and soak in the view. The water is clear enough for a dip if you like, though it’s more rocky than sandy beach. Highlights • One-of-a-kind natural rock formations + breathtaking sea views. • Peaceful and less crowded than central beaches. • Affordable entry, great for families, couples, or anyone wanting a chill escape. • Amazing sunset photo spots. Minor Drawbacks • Can get hot and dusty at midday if walking a lot. • Not a classic white-sand swimming beach (some seaweed). • Busier on weekends or peak tourist season—café seats fill up fast. Overall Rating I give it 8.5/10 — a beautiful, meaningful natural site with cultural charm (the Hòn Chồng legend) and a very relaxing vibe. Tripadvisor usually around 4/5 from hundreds of reviews, Google Maps similar—people rave about it being “peaceful,” “romantic,” and a “great sunset spot.” If you’re in Nha Trang, head there in the late afternoon for the best experience!

reviews.author: M C
reviews.text: It's a truly beautiful place. If you want to take pictures due to the large number of people, I recommend visiting early in the morning or late in the afternoon.

reviews.author: SUPRANEE SRIBOONSONG
reviews.text: Almost no place looks like the pictures — I’m so confused. It feels like I’ve been deceived. This place is full of tour buses, three or four arriving at once, and it’s just a rocky area. I don’t understand why this is considered a tourist attraction.

reviews.author: Danny Ng
reviews.text: 30k per pax for entrance. I believe the performance is a must watch, but it needs to wait for enough visitors to start performing. Each session is 3-4 songs and indeed good shows. Rocks and sea views (windy & hot) also good and worth to drop by. Grab to here and back for each way from Comodo hotel is around 90k per way. Drinks at the cafe is so so and a bit pricey and I think to drop by for a rest is okay but not for good drinks.

reviews.author: James L
reviews.text: Beautiful ocean view easy n short trail. Pic spot must visit

reviews.author: John Winter
reviews.text: A striking spot right on the Nha Trang coastline, Hòn Chồng features a stunning cluster of giant granite rocks that spill into the turquoise water. The rocks have weathered shapes and smooth surfaces, perfect for a photo op or just soaking up the coastal views. There’s more to it than just rocks: walkways lead you to a cultural area with traditional artwork and occasional live music performances, creating a blend of nature and culture that’s unique to this place. The water near the rocks is crystal clear, and you can see straight through to the pebbles beneath, beautiful on a sunny day. The small ticket fee (30,000 VND per person) is well worth it. It’s a great place to relax, snap photos, and even catch a glimpse of some locals fishing nearby. Tip: Try to go earlier in the day if you want to avoid the big tour groups, it can get crowded by mid-morning. We started to see the busses arrived at 9:30 am.

reviews.author: troy ware
reviews.text: If you are a huge child at heart you will love this spot. So many awesome rocks and crevasses to jump, climb and scale over like you did as a kid. A lot of great photo opportunities even if you are not a wild child, there is an easy walking path out to the main rock we will call it.

reviews.author: ALOUETTE ALEX
reviews.text: Nice view:)it depends on the weather if it’s sunny or cloudy. But the little gift shop over there,i do not think it’s a good place to buy souvenirs, kind of expensive:( and the staff was so mean to me:( The toilet is kind of smelly and it is for both of genders . The traditional music performances are nice but as the review i see before,if you just wanna enjoy the traditional music,you can come here!Here are just stacks of rockkkkkssssssssssssss:)

reviews.author: Elaine
reviews.text: amazing view! wind can also be breezy here so it's nice to chill. there's a way from the temple going down the rock part.

reviews.author: Shawn C
reviews.text: 30,000 for the entrance ticket the place is chill but packed. Recommand coming when it's not too busy.

reviews.author: Emma Ruan
reviews.text: A must-visit place when you come to Nha Trang. This place has mant rocks which naturally stacked on top of each other, running from the shore to the sea, creating an impressive natural layout with beautiful and breezy natural beaches. This place has ancient houses display picture and especially traditional musical instrument performance. Ticket: 30k/person

reviews.author: N L
reviews.text: I'm of 2 minds about this place. It is a very cool rock formation and there's a lot of history there. However, it is now a big tourist attraction that you have to pay to see it. Worst of all, you can't even go up close to the rock to see the "handprint".

reviews.author: Alberto Esteban Fernández
reviews.text: Not free, worth to pass by but not need to stop there. Have to pay 30k vnd to enter to a cafe to be able to go to the cliff, packed of people.

reviews.author: Tea Kemesic
reviews.text: Beautiful place! I came there around 2pm and there weren’t many people but around 4pm there were many so come during lunch time, it will probably be empty.

reviews.author: Lisa Waite
reviews.text: I guess if you’re in Nha Trang, this place with its rock formations is a must see and because of its historical significance was granted as a UNESCO Heritage site. There is also a cultural show here that highlights some of the traditional musical instruments. We love how beautiful the music is with these instruments and even a non traditional song sounds beautiful when played with these instruments 😉

reviews.author: Geoff Edwards
reviews.text: Beutiful... But if disabled Coukd be a bit difficult getting down to the rocks. As it stairs all the way.. But the rocks a scenery are lovely with a beach... There somewhere to buy gifts and have drinks.. 30dong.not expensive for the sights. Estimated time spent there. About a hour....

reviews.author: Alina Mertoiu
reviews.text: The view is beautiful there. It is a nice place to have a walk to. Beware, you can only pay cash and the closest ATM is at ~8 minutes away. The price was 30.000 dng

reviews.author: Michael B
reviews.text: Nice viewpoint that was better than expected. Nice rock formations and 270 degree view of the bays. Coffee shop at the entrance. Drinks are normal. However the ca phe phin coffee doesn't taste good quality

reviews.author: Warren Fenn
reviews.text: Very picturesque location, we heard some local cultural music and instruments inside. Rocks can be slippery if it's raining. A lot of tourists too, but some great trees, views and photo opps there.

reviews.author: YaR P
reviews.text: Nice place for visit, entry fee 30.000

reviews.author: Tallow Marble
reviews.text: Alot of Russians, busy beach but still worth visiting 👌👍

reviews.author: Peter C Byron (Hitchhiker007)
reviews.text: Beautiful traditional music. The rocks are a little under whelming but the restaurant area isn't too bad. The security is a little harsh and due to not being at the exit gate all the time allowing people to enter by mistake.

reviews.author: Ethan Brooke
reviews.text: A very unique landscape with some pretty views. If you have the time to visit, it’s worth it! You will need to pay a 30,000VND entrance fee, but that’s very affordable and it’s worth it. I recommend visiting at sunset if you can! The only real downside are the other tourists. Depending on when you visit, it can be very busy and loud. Also, once the sun goes down you will be kicked out, so don’t expect to stay too long after sunset.

reviews.author: Sean Hyland
reviews.text: Really cool viewpoint. Highly recommend for sunset 🌅

reviews.author: Kien Thuy Pham
reviews.text: And ok place for rock shots if you are limited for time. 22,000VND ticket price. Wear sneakers or shoes with good grip, as the rocks are sandy and slippery.... go there in flip flops at your own risk.The rocks are nice, but the view just average. Better to take the coastal road to just a few km north, and get gorgeous photos at Nha Phu Bay instead. Nui Hon Chong is an ideal place on the mountain, lies next to the East Sea. You can stay here to enjoy fresh seafood, view beautiful beach, have a sunbath, swim. A very pleasant place to walk along the promenade and stop for an evening photo session. After paying a small entrance fee, you enter the courtyard of a Vietnamese house, where a small concert is held every hour using traditional Vietnamese musical instruments. After going down the steps, you find yourself at the foot of small rocks sticking out of the water. Very attractive view for good photos. Come at sunset, you won't regret it !!!!

reviews.author: Paul Parent
reviews.text: Hòn Chồng, located in Nha Trang, is a striking natural landmark where massive granite boulders meet the sea in dramatic formations. This rocky outcrop, shaped by centuries of waves and wind, is both a geological wonder and a cultural symbol. Local legends surround the site, with stories adding mythical meaning to the giant hand-shaped mark said to be imprinted on one of the rocks. For photographers, Hòn Chồng is an inspiring location. The rugged stones against the deep blue of the ocean create strong contrasts, while the backdrop of Nha Trang Bay and nearby islands adds depth to wide-angle shots. Sunrise and sunset provide the most atmospheric conditions, with golden light highlighting the textures of the rocks and casting long reflections over the water. The area is easy to reach and includes nearby cafés and viewpoints where visitors can enjoy the scenery at a relaxed pace. Local fishing activity around the rocks also offers cultural elements to include in storytelling photography. Hòn Chồng is more than just a collection of rocks—it is a place where nature, legend, and daily coastal life come together. For travelers and photographers in Nha Trang, it remains a must-visit destination that captures the essence of Vietnam’s coastal beauty.

reviews.author: z. zidane
reviews.text: It's nice to just take a quick look around the sea and the rocks and then leave.

reviews.author: Lera
reviews.text: Beautiful place with various large stones. I went there on a weekday, but there were still many tourists. The entrance fee costs ₫30,000. It's better to wear sneakers, as it's more challenging to climb those stones in slippers
